architecture_summary: VM-UNet is a pure SSM-based U-shape architecture for medical image segmentation that uses VSS blocks from VMamba as the core module, with a patch embedding encoder, patch merging/expanding, additive skip connections, and an asymmetric encoder-decoder design. It is initialized with pretrained Vmamba-S weights and uses simple BCE/Dice or CE/Dice losses for segmentation.

training_details.compute_surface: All experiments are conducted on a single NVIDIA RTX A6000 GPU.

novel_contributions: We propose VM-UNet, marking the first occasion of exploring the potential applications of purely SSM-based models in medical image segmentation.

We establish a baseline for pure SSM-based models in medical image segmentation tasks, providing valuable insights that pave the way for the development of more efficient and effective SSM-based methods in medical image.
Comprehensive experiments are conducted on three datasets, with results indicating that VM-UNet exhibits considerable competitiveness.

key_claims.claim: VM-UNet is presented as the first pure SSM-based model for medical image segmentation.
VM-UNet uses VSS blocks as the foundation block and an asymmetrical encoder-decoder structure with fewer convolution layers to save calculation cost.
The paper reports competitive performance on ISIC17, ISIC18, and Synapse.
VM-UNet achieves the best results in four metrics on ISIC17 and three metrics on ISIC18 in Table I.
key_claims.evidence_section: IV. EXPERIMENTS / C. Comparison to the State-of-the-art Models

key_claims.claim: On Synapse, VM-UNet achieves 81.08 DSC and 19.21 HD95, and the text states it improves over Swin-UNet by 1.95% and 2.34mm in DSC and HD95.
key_claims.evidence_section: IV. EXPERIMENTS / C. Comparison to the State-of-the-art Models

key_claims.claim: Initializing VM-UNet with VMamba-S pretrained weights improves downstream performance compared with no pretrained weights or VMamba-T.
The asymmetric architecture {2,2,2,2-2,2,2,1} is chosen because symmetric or larger variants increase parameters/FLOPs and reduce performance.
The paper uses simple BCE/Dice or CE/Dice losses, random flip and random rotation augmentation, and trains for 300 epochs with batch size 32.
key_claims.evidence_section: III. METHODS / D. Loss Function; IV. EXPERIMENTS / B. Implementation Details
